5.reason: gets is unsafe and removed in C11, causing compilation error in strict environments.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  gets is unsafe and removed in C11, causing compilation error in strict environments.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  gets is unsafe and may cause compilation error in C11; use fgets instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  gets is deprecated in C99 and removed in C11, which causes a compilation error in modern strict compilers. Use fgets or scanf instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Compilation Error] The 'gets' function was removed from the C11 standard and causes compilation errors in modern environments. It should be replaced with 'fgets(s, sizeof(s), stdin);'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many compilers no longer support it, leading to a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many modern compilers (like GCC) no longer support it by default, leading to a compilation error. Use 'fgets' instead.]

12.reason: When t==a, s[a+1] is out of bounds; should loop t<a.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  When t==a, s[a+1] is out of bounds; should loop t<a.

101.reason: Does not check if s[i] is a digit; operator characters like '*' will corrupt the number.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Does not check if s[i] is a digit; operator characters like '*' will corrupt the number.
